title: baccharis multibracteata
intro: Baccharis multibracteata, also known as 'River Saltbush' or 'River Saltbush Baccharis', is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its narrow leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Baccharis multibracteata include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
